step_by_step_thinking: Assistant: I agree with agent1's conclusion. Both zinc oxide-eugenol and polycarboxylate cements achieve bonding to tooth structures primarily through chelation—the interaction of acidic functional groups (like those in polyacrylic acid or eugenol derivatives) with calcium ions in hydroxyapatite. This makes chelation the most accurate and specific common property among the listed options.
